title: Confirmation Dialog
description: Modal dialog asking the user to confirm or cancel a single action before it proceeds.
use_cases: confirm deletion of an item or record
approve an irreversible operation (publish, send, deploy)
warn before discarding unsaved changes
confirm a bulk action on multiple selected items

sections.when: User must explicitly approve or reject a single action. The dialog blocks interaction until resolved.
sections.not_for: Multi-step wizards (use Stepper), forms requiring user input beyond a checkbox (use Dialog directly), or non-blocking notifications (use Toaster).

steps: Wrap your app in ThemeProvider from @gravity-ui/uikit if not already done

items.name: ConfirmDialog
items.role: Ready-made confirm/cancel modal with layout, focus trap, backdrop, and built-in apply/cancel buttons. Props: open, onClose, title, message, textButtonApply, textButtonCancel, onClickButtonApply, onClickButtonCancel, propsButtonApply, propsButtonCancel.

items.name: Dialog
items.role: Lower-level modal for cases where the dialog body needs custom content (inputs, checkboxes, lists). Props: open, onClose, size ('s'|'m'|'l'), hasCloseButton, disableEscapeKeyDown, disableOutsideClick. Use sub-components Dialog.Header, Dialog.Body, Dialog.Footer.

items.name: Button
items.role: Trigger element that opens the dialog. Use view='outlined-danger' for destructive actions, view='action' for primary actions. Props: view, size, loading, disabled, onClick.

tree: textButtonApply   -- label for the confirm button
textButtonCancel  -- label for the cancel button
flow: User clicks trigger Button -> setState open=true
ConfirmDialog renders with title, message, and action buttons
User clicks Cancel -> onClickButtonCancel -> setState open=false
User clicks Apply -> onClickButtonApply -> run async action (set loading on apply button) -> on success close dialog -> show Toaster feedback
User clicks backdrop or presses Escape -> onClose -> setState open=false

sections.title: Simple delete confirmation with loading state
Custom body with Dialog (checkbox inside confirmation)

items: Custom modal overlay with manual backdrop and focus trapping -- ConfirmDialog handles backdrop, focus trap, and Escape key automatically
Hand-rolled confirm/cancel button pair inside a custom div -- use built-in textButtonApply/textButtonCancel props
window.confirm() -- cannot be styled, blocks the main thread, no loading states
Inline expand/collapse confirmation instead of a modal -- breaks user expectation for destructive actions, easy to miss

items.note: After the confirmed action completes, use Toaster to show success or error feedback.
If the dialog triggers an async operation, handle loading/error states within the dialog using Button loading prop.

title: Data Table with Filters and Actions
description: A full-featured data table with search filtering, column sorting, row selection, bulk actions via a floating panel, per-row context menus, and pagination. Designed for admin panels, dashboards, and list views where users browse, filter, and act on tabular data.
use_cases: browsing and managing a list of entities (users, orders, deployments, resources)
filtering table rows by keyword or status
selecting multiple rows and applying bulk actions (delete, export, assign)
navigating large datasets with pagination
performing per-row actions via a context menu (edit, copy, delete)

sections.when: You need a paginated table where users search/filter rows, select items for bulk operations, and trigger per-row actions. Data is loaded from an API or local state and may span many pages.
sections.not_for: Simple read-only key-value display (use DefinitionList). Tree/hierarchical data (use TreeSelect). Fewer than 5 items with card-like layout (use Card list). Editable inline cells (use a spreadsheet-like component or custom solution).

steps: Wrap your app in ThemeProvider from @gravity-ui/uikit if not already done

items.name: Table
items.role: Core data table with column configuration, row click handling, sorting support, and optional sticky horizontal scroll. Accepts columns (TableColumnConfig[]) and data (any[]) props.

items.name: TextInput
items.role: Search/filter input above the table. Use with hasClear prop and a magnifying glass left icon for search UX. Binds to filter state that controls which rows are displayed.

items.name: Select
items.role: Status or category filter dropdown placed alongside the search input. Use filterable prop for long option lists. Allows narrowing data by a specific column value.

items.name: Pagination
items.role: Pagination control below the table. Requires page, pageSize, total, and onUpdate props. Supports pageSizeOptions for user-adjustable page sizes and showInput for direct page navigation.

items.name: DropdownMenu
items.role: Per-row context menu rendered in the last column. Provides actions like Edit, Duplicate, Delete. Accepts items array with text, action, and optional theme ('danger' for destructive).

items.name: ActionsPanel
items.role: Floating panel that appears at the bottom when one or more rows are selected. Displays bulk action buttons (Delete selected, Export, Assign). Supports onClose to deselect all and renderNote to show selection count.

items.name: Checkbox
items.role: Row selection checkboxes rendered in the first column. Header checkbox toggles select-all for the current page. Controlled via selectedIds state.

items.name: Label
items.role: Status badges inside table cells (e.g. Active, Inactive, Pending). Use theme prop for color coding: 'success' for active, 'danger' for errors, 'warning' for pending, 'normal' for default.

tree: Select  (value=statusFilter, onUpdate, options, placeholder='All statuses', width=200)
column[0]: Checkbox  (header: select-all, cell: per-row toggle)
column[1..N]: data columns  (name, template for custom rendering)
ActionsPanel  (actions=[Delete, Export], onClose=clearSelection, renderNote='N items selected')
flow: 1. Raw data loaded from API or props into state: allData[]
2. Filter stage: allData filtered by search text (TextInput) and status (Select) -> filteredData[]
3. Sort stage: filteredData sorted by active sort column and direction -> sortedData[]
4. Pagination stage: sortedData sliced by (page-1)*pageSize to page*pageSize -> paginatedRows[]
5. Table renders paginatedRows with columns config
6. Checkbox column manages selectedIds: Set<string> state
7. Select-all checkbox toggles all paginatedRows IDs into or out of selectedIds
8. When selectedIds.size > 0, ActionsPanel appears with bulk actions
9. Per-row DropdownMenu dispatches actions for a single row
10. Pagination onUpdate resets page when pageSize changes, preserves selectedIds across pages

sections.title: Full data table with search filter, status filter, row selection, bulk actions, and pagination

items: Filtering data inside the render path without useMemo -- causes unnecessary re-computation on every render. Always memoize filter, sort, and pagination stages separately.
Storing filtered/sorted data in state instead of deriving it -- creates stale-data bugs when the source data or filters change. Derive from allData + filter + sort params.
Building a custom pagination component -- Pagination from @gravity-ui/uikit handles page numbers, page size selector, and edge cases. Use pageSizeOptions and showInput props.
Using window.confirm() for bulk delete -- use ConfirmDialog or Dialog for confirmation with proper loading states and async handling.
Putting DropdownMenu outside the column template -- the menu must be scoped to the row. Render it inside the column's template function so it receives the correct row context.
Resetting selectedIds on every page change -- users expect cross-page selection. Only clear selection explicitly via ActionsPanel onClose or after a bulk action completes.
Hardcoding page size without pageSizeOptions -- let users choose between 10, 20, 50, 100 items per page to accommodate different screen sizes and workflows.

items.note: Use ConfirmDialog before executing destructive bulk actions like delete.
After bulk actions complete, show a Toaster notification with success or error status.
When filteredData is empty, consider showing an EmptyState illustration instead of just the emptyMessage string.

title: File Upload with Preview
description: Drag-and-drop file upload area with image/file previews, upload progress indicators, and remove actions. Gravity UI provides FilePreview and Progress atoms but has no built-in dropzone component, so the drop area and upload logic are custom.
use_cases: upload one or more files via drag-and-drop or file picker
show thumbnail previews for images and type icons for other files
display per-file upload progress with cancel/retry actions
attach files to a form (e.g. support ticket, profile avatar, document submission)

sections.when: Users need to upload files with visual feedback: thumbnails, progress bars, and the ability to remove files before or during upload.
sections.not_for: Single-click download links (use Button with href). Pasting rich content into a text editor (use the editor's built-in paste handler). Server-side file import from another system (no UI needed).

steps: Wrap your app in ThemeProvider from @gravity-ui/uikit if not already done
Optional: install a dedicated library like react-dropzone for robust drag-and-drop handling

items.name: FilePreview
items.role: Renders a preview tile for each file showing name, type icon or image thumbnail, and optional action buttons (e.g. remove).

items.name: Progress
items.role: Shows per-file upload progress as a horizontal bar. Use value (0-100), theme for status coloring, and text to display percentage.

items.name: Button
items.role: Fallback 'Browse files' trigger for users who prefer clicking over drag-and-drop. Also used for retry/cancel actions on individual files.

items.name: Icon
items.role: Renders SVG icons from @gravity-ui/icons inside the dropzone area and on action buttons (TrashBin, ArrowUpFromLine, Xmark, ArrowRotateLeft).

items.name: Text
items.role: Typography for dropzone instructions, file size labels, and error messages. Use variant and color props for visual hierarchy.

items.name: Card
items.role: Container wrapper around the dropzone area. Use view='outlined' with a dashed border override for the idle state.

items.name: Label
items.role: Status badge on each file item (e.g. 'Uploading', 'Done', 'Error'). Use theme='success' for completed, theme='danger' for failed.

items.name: Alert
items.role: Inline error message when a file exceeds the size limit or has an unsupported type.

items.name: Toaster
items.role: Toast notification after all files finish uploading or when an upload fails.

items.name: DropZone
items.description: A div that handles onDragEnter, onDragOver, onDragLeave, onDrop events and visually highlights when files are dragged over it.
items.approach: Custom <div> with drag event handlers and a hidden <input type='file'> for the click-to-browse fallback. Toggle a CSS class on drag-over to show a highlight border. Alternatively, use react-dropzone's useDropzone hook for cross-browser robustness.

items.name: Upload logic (useFileUpload hook)
items.description: State management for the file queue: per-file status (pending, uploading, done, error), progress percentage, abort controllers, and retry capability.
items.approach: Custom React hook that wraps XMLHttpRequest or fetch with ReadableStream to track upload progress. Stores an array of file entries with {id, file, status, progress, error, abortController}.

items.name: File validation
items.description: Client-side checks for allowed MIME types, max file size, and max file count before uploading.
items.approach: A validate function run on each file in the drop/select handler. Returns an error string or null. Rejected files are shown with Alert or inline error text.

tree: FileUploadWithPreview  (organism root, manages file list state)
Alert?  (validation errors: wrong type, too large)
FileList  (custom wrapper, maps over file entries)
Button?  (flat, Icon data={ArrowRotateLeft}, retry on error)
flow: User drags files over DropZone -> onDragEnter sets highlight state, zone border changes to dashed primary color
User drops files (or clicks Browse and selects via file picker) -> onDrop/onChange fires
Validate each file (type, size, count) -> rejected files trigger Alert with reason, accepted files added to state
For image files, call URL.createObjectURL(file) to generate imageSrc for FilePreview thumbnail
Start upload for each accepted file -> set status='uploading', create AbortController
XHR onprogress / fetch ReadableStream updates entry.progress (0-100) -> Progress re-renders
Upload success -> set status='done', Progress theme='success', show Label theme='success'
Upload failure -> set status='error', Progress theme='danger', show retry Button
User clicks remove (TrashBin action on FilePreview) -> abort if uploading, remove entry from state, revoke object URL
User clicks retry -> reset status='uploading', progress=0, re-initiate upload
All files done -> optional Toaster notification with success summary

sections.title: Complete file upload with drag-and-drop, previews, and progress
Minimal single-file upload with compact preview

items: Using a bare <input type='file'> without visual dropzone -- poor UX, no drag-and-drop support, no preview
Building a custom file preview card from scratch when FilePreview already handles file name, type icon, image thumbnails, and action buttons
Polling the server for upload progress -- use XHR upload.onprogress or fetch ReadableStream for real-time client-side progress
Storing File objects in Redux/global state -- File objects are not serializable; keep them in local component state or refs
Forgetting to call URL.revokeObjectURL when removing image previews -- causes memory leaks
Using a single Progress bar for multiple file uploads -- show per-file progress so users can identify which file is slow or failed
Accepting all file types without validation -- always validate MIME type and file size before uploading to prevent server errors and security issues

items.note: Use a confirmation dialog before removing a file that has already finished uploading.
Show a Toaster notification when all uploads complete or when an upload fails.

title: Page States: Loading, Empty, Error
description: Branching pattern that renders the correct visual state for a page or section depending on whether data is loading, the result set is empty, an error occurred, or content is ready.
use_cases: show a skeleton placeholder while page data is fetching
display a centered spinner for lazy-loaded sections
present an empty-state illustration when query returns zero results
render an error message with a retry action after a failed request
gate content rendering behind a loading/error/empty check

sections.when: A page, panel, or section fetches async data and must show distinct UI for loading, empty, error, and success states.
sections.not_for: Inline loading inside a button or label (use Spin directly), optimistic UI updates that skip the loading state, or skeleton-only table rows handled by the table component itself.

matrix.situation: Full page is loading for the first time
matrix.why: Skeleton preserves layout shape and reduces perceived load time by showing content placeholders in place.

matrix.situation: A small section or widget is loading
matrix.why: Loader is a centered animated spinner suitable for contained areas where layout shape is unknown.

matrix.situation: Inline element inside a button or label is loading
matrix.why: Spin is a compact rotating semicircle designed to sit inline next to text without breaking line height.

matrix.situation: Data loaded successfully but the result set is empty
matrix.why: PlaceholderContainer provides a structured empty-state layout with image, title, description, and optional action buttons.
PlaceholderContainer doubles as an error state by setting a relevant title, description, and a retry action button.

steps: Wrap your app in ThemeProvider from @gravity-ui/uikit if not already done

items.name: Skeleton
items.role: Displays a placeholder preview that mimics content layout while data loads. Set dimensions via style prop. Supports 'gradient' (default), 'pulse', or 'none' animation.

items.name: PlaceholderContainer
items.role: Structured empty-state or error-state block with image, title, description, and action buttons. Used for both empty results and error fallbacks.

items.name: Loader
items.role: Animated spinner for section-level loading where the layout shape is unknown or a centered indicator is preferred over skeleton blocks.

items.name: Spin
items.role: Compact inline spinner (rotating semicircle) for use inside buttons, labels, or other inline elements. Not for page-level loading.

flow: isError? -> PlaceholderContainer (error image, title='Something went wrong', description=error.message, actions=[{text:'Retry', onClick:refetch}])
isLoading? -> Skeleton rows matching content layout  OR  Loader centered in container
data is empty? -> PlaceholderContainer (empty image, title='No results', description='Try adjusting your filters', actions=[{text:'Clear filters', onClick:resetFilters}])
else -> render actual content

sections.title: Page state branching with Skeleton, PlaceholderContainer, and error retry
Section loading with Loader spinner instead of Skeleton

items: Showing a blank white page while data loads -- use Skeleton or Loader to indicate progress and reduce perceived wait time
Using Spin as a full-page loading indicator -- Spin is designed for inline scenarios; use Skeleton or Loader for page/section loading
Rendering content immediately and then replacing it when data arrives -- causes layout shift; gate rendering behind the loading check
Showing a generic 'Loading...' text string instead of a visual indicator -- Skeleton and Loader provide consistent branded experience
Forgetting the empty state and rendering an empty container or blank table -- always show PlaceholderContainer with guidance
Displaying a raw error object or stack trace to the user -- wrap errors in PlaceholderContainer with a human-readable message and retry action
Checking states in the wrong order (content before error) -- always check error first, then loading, then empty, then content

items.note: After a retry succeeds, use Toaster to confirm the data loaded successfully.
If the error state offers a destructive fallback action, confirm it with a ConfirmDialog first.

title: Theming and Dark Mode Setup
description: Foundation recipe for configuring Gravity UI theming, wrapping your app in ThemeProvider, and building a theme toggle that supports light, dark, and system-preference modes.
use_cases: set up Gravity UI theming from scratch in a new project
add a dark mode toggle to an existing application
respect the user's OS-level color scheme preference
scope a different theme to a specific section of the page

sections.when: You are starting a new Gravity UI project, or adding theme switching to an existing one. ThemeProvider must wrap all Gravity UI components — it injects the CSS custom properties (--g-color-*) that every component depends on.
sections.not_for: Per-component color overrides (use className or style props instead). Branding that only changes the accent color (override --g-color-base-brand in CSS). Server-side-only pages with no client React tree.

steps: Import the required CSS at the top of your entry file: import '@gravity-ui/uikit/styles/fonts.css'; import '@gravity-ui/uikit/styles/styles.css';
Wrap your root component in ThemeProvider: import {ThemeProvider} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';
Set the theme prop to 'light', 'dark', or 'system' — 'system' follows the user's prefers-color-scheme media query
Use the useTheme() hook anywhere inside the provider to read or change the current theme at runtime

items.name: ThemeProvider
items.role: Root provider that injects CSS custom properties (--g-color-base-background, --g-color-text-primary, etc.) onto a wrapper element. Accepts theme prop: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'. Can be nested for scoped theming.

items.name: useTheme
items.role: Hook returning [theme, setTheme]. theme is the resolved value ('light' | 'dark'). setTheme accepts 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'.

items.name: Switch
items.role: Simple two-state toggle, suitable for a light/dark toggle when system mode is not offered. Props: checked, onUpdate, size ('s' | 'm' | 'l').

items.name: RadioGroup
items.role: Three-option selector for light / dark / system. Props: options (RadioGroupOption[]), value, onUpdate, direction ('horizontal' | 'vertical'), size ('m' | 'l' | 'xl').

items.name: ThemeToggle (Switch variant)
items.description: A composed component that uses Switch to flip between light and dark. Reads current theme from useTheme() and calls setTheme on toggle.

items.name: ThemeSelector (RadioGroup variant)
items.description: A composed component that uses RadioGroup to choose between light, dark, and system. The system option defers to the OS prefers-color-scheme.

tree: Main content  (all Gravity UI components inherit theme)

sections.title: Full app setup with system theme and toggle
Scoped dark section inside a light app
Persisting theme preference to localStorage

items: Toggling a .dark-theme CSS class on the body manually -- ThemeProvider manages the CSS custom properties; duplicating this leads to conflicts and flickering
Importing individual component CSS files instead of the main styles.css -- the main stylesheet includes all component styles and theme token definitions
Hardcoding color hex values instead of using --g-color-* tokens -- hardcoded values will not respond to theme changes
Using window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') directly -- ThemeProvider with theme='system' handles this automatically, including listening for changes
Wrapping every component in its own ThemeProvider -- one root provider is sufficient; nest only when you need a scoped theme override
Forgetting the CSS imports (fonts.css, styles.css) -- components will render without any styles or theme tokens

items.note: Dialogs inherit the current theme from ThemeProvider automatically.
Toaster inherits theme context; no extra setup needed for themed notifications.
